summary: We are in a high-velocity execution phase, simultaneously redesigning the knowledge website, launching the Asia Start campaign with an A/B test, building the Intelligent China program, and co-creating a major joint report on Chinese companies going global—all while prioritizing speed in agency selection and SEO.
summaryDetail: The most urgent initiative is the knowledge website redesign, where we have decided to combine Joker's and Joseph's designs to show more articles and bring indices to the homepage; feedback and HTML files are due this week. The Asia Start campaign has just launched with two parallel routes (AI vs traditional) for the first month, and the Intelligent China program webpage must go live this week, with the SCM campaign planned. The joint report with IE on Chinese companies going global to Southeast Asia is a major Q3 priority, with a working title 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global', targeting a September launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson events in Singapore; Felix is drafting the one-pager and confirming event partnerships. SEO strategy is focused on ranking for AI, EVs, and smart manufacturing in China, with Joseph providing a brief on Google Ads conversion tracking and a list of target search areas. Social media agency selection is being expedited, with a second round of bidding materials due this week. Meanwhile, the team is handling brochure updates, press release streamlining, and follow-ups on the CKGSB x Tech In Asia partnership and UNICON membership.

meetings.title: Knowledge Website Redesign Review
meetings.summary: Team reviewed Joker's redesign of the knowledge website, finding it too similar to current version. They discussed Joseph's alternative layout for inspiration and agreed to combine elements from both, focusing on showing more articles and bringing indices to the homepage.

meetings.title: SEO & Program Prioritization Strategy
meetings.summary: Team discusses SEO strategy to rank for AI, EVs, and smart manufacturing in China, prioritizing Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting
meetings.summary: Team discussed feedback on menu categorization, brochure updates, CES quotes, Ding Hight Hall video, Asia Start articles, Davos press release, and social media agency search. Decided to streamline Shun Tian Shu press release and expedite agency selection.

meetings.title: Joint Report on Chinese Companies Going Global
meetings.summary: Meeting to plan a jointly published report between CKGSB and IE on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, with op-eds from both schools and case studies. Report to launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson event in Singapore in mid-September.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Reviewed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional), discussed new Intelligent China program targeting foreigners with modules in Beijing, Shanghai, Hangzhou, Shenzhen.

meetings.title: EU-China Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on EU-China relations, focusing on geopolitical tensions, de-risking, and the role of companies versus governments in shaping cooperation.

decisions.text: Remove dedicated video section; videos will be dispersed by topic.
Combine elements from Joker's and Joseph's designs for the final homepage.
Show more articles on homepage, with pinned article at top left and latest articles below.
Bring indices (BCI, BSI) to the knowledge homepage.
Prioritize Asia Star and Intelligent China programs; put US partnerships on hold initially.
Intelligent China webpage to launch this week; Asia Star page already live.
Only one press release for Shun Tian Shu, incorporating 10 points.
Prioritize speed in social media agency selection; consider Media Minds.
Translate no more than 50% of IE report.
Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE, with main research from Professor Leeway and op-eds from both schools.
